title: mirabilis laevis var. villosa
intro: Mirabilis laevis var. villosa, also known as the hairy or woolly gilia, is a variety of the western gilia plant, a member of the phlox family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The medicinal properties of Mirabilis laevis var. villosa have been attributed to its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, making it a potential treatment for infections. Herbal preparations of the plant may include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its health benefits.
